title: Fix the Missing Edge Case in Binary Search

statement.markdown: The function `binarySearch` should find the index of a target element in a sorted array using binary search. However, the current implementation does not handle edge cases correctly. Implement a fix to make the function work for all valid inputs.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a sorted array of integers and a target integer.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return the index of the target element if it exists in the array, or -1 if it does not.
statement.notes: The function should not modify the input array.

examples.explanation: The target element 51 is at index 24 in the array.
The target element 100 is not present in the array, so the function returns -1.

hints.text: Consider the edge case where the target element is not present in the array.
Think about how you can modify the binary search algorithm to handle this edge case correctly.
